content:
# Wajid Sandstone

**Summary**: A major Cambrian-Ordovician clastic unit in southern Arabia.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] analyzed the Wajid Sandstone to determine its depositional history and sedimentary units.

- [[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017]] studied the Wajid Sandstone Group to characterize its fracture systems.

- [[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022]] identified the Wajh Formation as the underlying unit for Miocene carbonates.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] studied the Al Wajh Formation to evaluate its reservoir potential.

- [[2023-arrofi|Arrofi, 2023]] identified as one of the rock units in Zone C of the Jizan field.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] studied the Wajid Sandstone to determine its depositional history.

## Depositional Environment

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] fluviatile to fluviatile-marine

- [[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017]] studied the Wajid Sandstone Group to characterize its fracture systems.

- [[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022]] identified the Wajh Formation as the underlying unit for Miocene carbonates.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] studied the Al Wajh Formation to evaluate its reservoir potential.

- [[2023-arrofi|Arrofi, 2023]] identified as one of the rock units in Zone C of the Jizan field.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] studied the Wajid Sandstone to determine its depositional history.

## Reservoir Properties



## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Juwayl member of the Wajid Sandstone shows evidence of microchanneling and slumps, potentially indicating a periglacial climate. ([[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014, p. 75]])

- The formation acts as a groundwater aquifer and potential hydrocarbon reservoir. ([[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017, p. 1]])

- Composed of reddish-brown conglomerates within a sandstone matrix, unconformably overlying the basement. ([[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022, p. 38]])

- The formation contains four main facies associations: alluvial fan, braided fluvial, floodplain, and shoreface/delta front. ([[2023-butt|Butt, 2023, p. 74]])

- Zone C is located within several rock units including Wajid sandstone. ([[2023-arrofi|Arrofi, 2023, p. 83]])

- The formation consists of a Red Unit and a Grey Unit with distinct lithofacies and mineralogical signatures. ([[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999, p. 12]])

## Conflicting Interpretations Across Sources

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] microcrystalline quartz and calcite cementation
